heading: Influencing, impeding, or retaliating against a Federal official by threatening or injuring a family member
label: 115 Influencing, impeding, or retaliating against a Federal official by threatening or injuring a family member

(a) read as follows: “Whoever assaults, kidnaps, or murders, or attempts to kidnap or murder, or threatens to assault, kidnap or murder a member of the immediate family of a United States official, a United States judge, a Federal law enforcement officer, or an official whose killing would be a crime under , or threatens to assault, kidnap, or murder, a United States official, a United States judge, a Federal law enforcement officer, or an official whose killing would be a crime under such section with intent to impede, intimidate, interfere with, or retaliate against such official, judge or law enforcement officer while engaged in or on account of the performance of official duties, shall be punished as provided in subsection (b).”Pub. children.label: A
children.text: assaults, kidnaps, or murders, or attempts or conspires to kidnap or murder, or threatens to assault, kidnap or murder a member of the immediate family of a United States official, a United States judge, a Federal law enforcement officer, or an official whose killing would be a crime under ; orsection 1114 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: threatens to assault, kidnap, or murder, a United States official, a United States judge, a Federal law enforcement officer, or an official whose killing would be a crime under such section,

Whoever assaults, kidnaps, or murders, or attempts or conspires to kidnap or murder, or threatens to assault, kidnap, or murder, any person who formerly served as a person designated in paragraph (1), or a member of the immediate family of any person who formerly served as a person designated in paragraph (1), with intent to retaliate against such person on account of the performance of official duties during the term of service of such person, shall be punished as provided in subsection (b).

children.label: A
children.text: a fine under this title; and

children.label: i
children.text: if the assault consists of a simple assault, a term of imprisonment for not more than 1 year;

children.label: ii
children.text: if the assault involved physical contact with the victim of that assault or the intent to commit another felony, a term of imprisonment for not more than 10 years;

children.label: iii
children.text: if the assault resulted in bodily injury, a term of imprisonment for not more than 20 years; or

children.label: iv
children.text: if the assault resulted in serious bodily injury (as that term is defined in , and including any conduct that, if the conduct occurred in the special maritime and territorial jurisdiction of the United States, would violate section 2241 or 2242 of this title) or a dangerous weapon was used during and in relation to the offense, a term of imprisonment for not more than 30 years.section 1365 of this title

A kidnapping, attempted kidnapping, or conspiracy to kidnap in violation of this section shall be punished as provided in for the kidnapping or attempted kidnapping of, or a conspiracy to kidnap, a person described in .section 1201 of this titlesection 1201(a)(5) of this title
A murder, attempted murder, or conspiracy to murder in violation of this section shall be punished as provided in sections 1111, 1113, and 1117 of this title.

A threat made in violation of this section shall be punished by a fine under this title or imprisonment for a term of not more than 10 years, or both, except that imprisonment for a threatened assault shall not exceed 6 years.

“Federal law enforcement officer” means any officer, agent, or employee of the United States authorized by law or by a Government agency to engage in or supervise the prevention, detection, investigation, or prosecution of any violation of Federal criminal law;

children.label: A
children.text: his spouse, parent, brother or sister, child or person to whom he stands in loco parentis; or

children.label: B
children.text: any other person living in his household and related to him by blood or marriage;
“United States judge” means any judicial officer of the United States, and includes a justice of the Supreme Court and a United States magistrate judge; and

“United States official” means the President, President-elect, Vice President, Vice President-elect, a Member of Congress, a member-elect of Congress, a member of the executive branch who is the head of a department listed in , or the Director of the Central Intelligence Agency.5 U.S.C. 101

children.label: d
children.text: This section shall not interfere with the investigative authority of the United States Secret Service, as provided under sections 3056, 871, and 879 of this title.

children.label: e
children.text: There is extraterritorial jurisdiction over the conduct prohibited by this section.
